name: Animal Feed
description: Animal feed works like rations for animals. Each Camp Phase, you must feed each animal companion feed or they will become weak and stop following commands. Animal feed is specific to a particular animal (hound, falcon, horse, etc.).

name: Bedroll
description: Bedrolls consist of blankets and bedding materials. Without either a bedroll or a tent, sleeping on the cold ground makes you Stressed. (2 slots)

name: Candles
properties: Bright light for the holder, dim for the guild
Goes out if dropped (can be relit)
2 flickers of fuel
description: Candles provide bright light for the person carrying them, but don’t provide any light for those nearby. As such, they are used when the guild—as a whole— prefers stealth. Candles can be snuffed out and relit (whereas torches cannot be). Candles go out when dropped. Candles can flicker 2 times before they’re used up.

name: Chalk
description: Chalk can make marks almost anywhere—useful for plotting your way around a maze, warning fellow adventurers about traps, or making crude drawings of your rivals’ mothers.

name: Clothes, Rags
description:
Rags preserve your basic dignity, but make you look like the worst sort of peasant.

-

name: Firewood
description: Firewood allows you to build campfires even when in the deepest dungeon.

name: Flint and Tinder
description: Flint and tinder let you start fires.

name: Iron Spikes
description: Iron spikes can be used to wedge doors closed, serve as pitons for climbing, or be driven through the heart of the undead. (Stackable 6 per slot)

name: Lard
description: Lard is the fat of a pig. It burns well, can be used as a grease to lubricate tools and surfaces, and can even be used as soap. You can also eat it like a ration in a pinch to keep from starving.

name: Leeches, Jar of
properties: Camp: draw a card — Cups or Wands counts as 2 charges toward curing an affliction
description: Leeches draw out bad blood and let good yellow phlegm and bile reign in your poisoned body. You can apply leeches to another adventurer with the Use an Item Camp Action. If you do so, draw a card. If you draw a Swords or Pentacles card, nothing happens. If you draw a Cups or Wands card, the leeched adventurer counts as having burned 2 charges to cure an affliction.

name: Pipe and Pipeweed
properties: Smoke it all to clear Stressed
description: Pipe and pipeweed are a great way to relax, clear your mind, and make the camp smell nice. Smoking all of your pipeweed clears the Stressed condition.

name: Pole, 10'
description: 10’ poles are helpful, too-long walking sticks that you can use to prod and poke for traps and hazards. (Oversized)

name: Poultice
properties: Expend at Death's Door: test Wands (favor with a medical motif) to save them
description:
Poultices are bandages infused with healthful herbs. They have a few different uses:

- You can expend a poultice to try and save a character at Death’s Door. The injured character test Wands; this test has favor if the person applying it has a medical motif. If successful, the Death’s Door condition is cured and the character returns to consciousness. If it is a failure, the character dies. See Death’s Door for the full rules.

- Poultices are also necessary for the use of the Chirurgeon talent.

name: Rations
description: Rations consist of hardtack, dried fruit, and dried meats. It might not be delicious, but it’s better than starving. Each ration contains a canteen with a day’s worth of water or swill alcohol.

name: Religious Paraphernalia
properties: Brandish: test Wands to displace one undead or spirit
description: Religious paraphernalia is a catchall term for the icons, amulets, or rosaries used to ward away certain types of undead or spirits. If brandished, test Wands. If successful, Displace one affected creature. During a Challenge, brandishing a religious icon is a Wands action.

name: Rope
description: Rope comes in lengths of 50’. Every adventurer worth their salt carries a bit of rope on them.

name: Axe
properties: Take-down: downing a foe lets the same Attack value strike another enemy in the zone
Can be wielded two-handed
description: Axes include all types of chopping weapons, such as battle axes, bardiches, or mattocks. When an axe Attack takes down an enemy, you may use the same Attack value against another opponent in the same zone.

name: Blade
properties: Riposte deals 2 Wounds instead of 1
Can be wielded two-handed
description: Blades include all types of swords, from short swords to longswords. When you successfully Riposte with a blade, you deal 2 Wounds instead of 1 Wound.

name: Bow
properties: Ranged
Aim (Swords): bank a facedown card, reveal on the shot to add its value
Melee attackers gain favor against you
description:
Bows are ranged weapons that use arrows. When wielding a bow, you gain access to the special Aim Challenge Action.

- Aim is a Swords action. Declare who you’re targeting and play your card facedown. When you next Attack with your bow, you may reveal the card and add its value to your total Attack.

If you are attacked with a melee weapon while wielding a missile weapon, the attacker has favor against you.

name: Crossbow
properties: Ranged
All Attacks deal Piercing damage
Reload/crank as a miscellaneous action
Melee attackers gain favor against you
description:
Crossbows are mechanized ranged weapons with a locking mechanism to keep the bowstring taut. All Attacks with a crossbow deal Piercing damage. However, your crossbow must be reloaded and cranked as a miscellaneous action after firing.

If you are attacked with a melee weapon while wielding a missile weapon, the attacker has favor against you.

name: Dagger
properties: Piercing vs unarmed, Rooted, or Tripped targets
Throwable without engaging
description: Daggers are short, bladed weapons that include stilettos, gladiuses, and dirks. Daggers deal Piercing damage against targets who are unarmed, Rooted, or Tripped. Daggers may be thrown to Attack targets in your zone without engaging them.

name: Flail
properties: Attacks cannot be absorbed by shields
Always wins Initiative ties
Can be wielded two-handed
description: Flails include any flexible weapon, such as morning stars or ball-and-chain. Flail Attacks cannot be absorbed by shields and always win ties with Initiative.

name: Hammer / Mace
properties: Attack ≥ 2× the target's Initiative deals 2 Wounds
Can be wielded two-handed
description: Hammers and maces include any type of smashing weapon. When your Attack value is at least *twice* that of your opponent’s Initiative, your hammer deals 2 Wounds instead of 1 Wound.

name: Polearm
properties: Ripostes are interrupts
Two-handed
description: Polearms are long-hafted weapons that include spears, halberds, and quarterstaffs. Ripostes are interrupts when wielding a polearm. (2 slots)

name: Arrows
description: You must keep track of your arrows and bolts. They come in quivers of 12 per slot. Any arrow that hits a target is Destroyed (your opponent breaks off the shaft in combat, the bolt head is blunted on bone, etc.). Any arrow that misses may be recovered after the Challenge. Use the Fletch Arrows Camp Action to replace Destroyed arrows/bolts.

name: Bolts
description: You must keep track of your arrows and bolts. They come in quivers of 12 per slot. Any arrow that hits a target is Destroyed (your opponent breaks off the shaft in combat, the bolt head is blunted on bone, etc.). Any arrow that misses may be recovered after the Challenge. Use the Fletch Arrows Camp Action to replace Destroyed arrows/bolts.

name: Armor, Light
properties: Worn: takes 1 belt slot
Absorbs 1 Notch
description:
Light armor is designed for movement and comfort; it can typically be worn under normal clothes—or may have the appearance of fine garb. Common forms of light armor include leather jerkins, orcish furs, or bone armor. Light armor can absorb 1 Notch.

- Light armor takes up 1 belt slot.

name: Caltrops
properties: Scattered: unshod creatures crossing take a Wound
description: Caltrops are nails designed to be scattered on the ground. They deny areas to your foes, especially cavalry. When scattered, any unshod creature walking in the zone takes a Wound.

name: Chain
description: Chain comes in 10’ lengths per slot. Chain is useful for all sorts of bindings and haulings.

name: Clothes, Common
description:
Common clothes won’t make you popular at parties, but they’re serviceable and good for dirty work.

-

name: Cooking Gear
description: Cooking gear is needed to prepare hunted game into edible food during the Camp Phase.

name: Crowbar
properties: Pry doors and chests: test Swords
description: Crowbars are used to pry open doors and chests by testing Swords. Less subtle than a lockpick, doing so breaks the door in question.

name: Fishing Gear
description: Fishing gear is needed to fish and gather food on bodies of water—like in the deadly Boundless Moat.

name: Garlic
properties: Brandish: test Wands to displace the impure
description: Garlic wards away certain types of undead and impure spirits. If brandished, test Wands. If successful, Displace one affected creature. In a Challenge, brandishing garlic is a Wands action.

name: Grappling Hook
properties: With rope: climb sheer surfaces without testing fate
description: Grappling hooks, when combined with rope, let you climb sheer surfaces without testing fate. A grappling hook takes up its own slot, separate from the rope.

name: Hammer (tool)
description: Hammers are used to drive in spikes and pitons, break things that don’t want to be broken, and are generally useful tools. It should be noted that trying to smash things open with weapons generally results in a Notched or Destroyed weapon.

name: Hatchet
description: Hatchets are used to fell wood, cut ropes, and smash open doors. It should be noted that trying to smash things open with weapons generally results in a Notched or Destroyed weapon.

name: Helm
properties: Worn on the head (no belt slots)
Absorbs 1 Notch
description: Helms can turn aside deadly blows. A helm can absorb 1 Notch before being Destroyed. (1 belt slot)

name: Hermetic Bottle
properties: Fragile: destroyed after 1 Notch
description: Hermetic bottles keep alchemical reagents fresh and potent. They are used to hold alchemical substances like bombs, oils, and potions. An essential tool for the Alchemy talent.

name: Lockpicks
properties: Pick a lock: test Pentacles — failure snaps a pick
description: Lockpicks can be used to pick a lock with a successful test of Pentacles. If you fail, one of your lockpicks breaks. (Stackable 6 per slot)

name: Mirror
description: Mirrors are kept by those still trying to keep up a semblance of hygiene, or just to reflect a monster’s dread gaze back onto itself.

name: Musical Instrument
description: Musical instruments are often carried by adventurers, as song keeps campfires merry in a dark and oppressive environment. These could be lutes, harps, flutes, viols, drums, or anything else found in the Wide World.

name: Pick
description: Picks are tools used for digging. (Oversized)

name: Quill and Ink
description: Quill and ink are necessary tools for the journey into literary fame.

name: Shield, Light
properties: Held: ties in Challenges go to you, not the attacker
May swap Initiative for a hand card (miscellaneous action)
Absorbs 1 Notch
description:
Shields come in a variety of styles and methods of construction, but are considered either light or heavy. Shields take one of your hand slots.

- Light shields are made from wood. They can absorb 1 Notch.

- Heavy shields are made of metal or similarly sturdy materials and can absorb 2 Notches.

In a Challenge, ties go to the attacker unless the defender carries a shield.

If you have a shield, you may replace your Initiative with any card from your hand as a miscellaneous action. Your old Initiative is discarded.

name: Shovel
description: Shovels are tools used for digging. Like picks. But different. (Oversized)

name: Tinker's Kit
properties: Camp: remove 1 Notch from an item (can't salvage the Destroyed)
description: Tinker’s kits are metal-working tools and scrap metal. You can repair damaged equipment with a tinker’s kit by performing the Use an Item Camp Action to remove 1 Notch. Destroyed equipment cannot be salvaged in this manner. (2 slots)

name: Torch
properties: Bright light for the holder, dim for the guild
Dropped: 50% chance it dies (and can't be relit)
3 flickers of fuel
description: Torches provide bright light to the person holding them and dim light for everybody else in the guild. Torches have a 50% chance to go out when dropped. Torches that are extinguished are almost impossible to relight. Torches can flicker 3 times before they’re used up.

name: Wolfsbane
properties: Brandish: test Wands to displace wolves and lycanthropes
description: Wolfsbane repels wolves and lycanthropes. If brandished, test Wands. If successful, Displace one affected creature. During a Challenge, brandishing wolfsbane is a Wands action.

name: Alchemy Kit
description: Alchemy kits are used to mix reagents into alchemical substances (bombs, potions, oils). They are a required tool for the Alchemy talent. (2 slots)

name: Armor, Iron
properties: Worn: takes 2 belt slots
Absorbs 2 Notches
Spells cannot target the wearer (spell effects still apply)
No swimming, sneaking, or spellcasting
description:
Iron armor consists of a coat of iron mail worn over a padded gambeson. Iron armor can absorb 2 Notches.

- Iron armor is the bane of sorcerers—spells cannot target anyone wearing iron armor; however, you can still be hurt by the effects of spells (e.g., burned by a wall of fire).

- You cannot cast spells if wearing iron armor.

- Iron armor takes up 2 belt slots while worn.

- Due to iron’s weight, it is impossible to swim or sneak while wearing iron armor.

name: Armor, Steel
properties: Worn: takes 3 belt slots
Absorbs 3 Notches
No swimming, sneaking, or spellcasting
description:
Steel armor is a full set of steel plate. Steel does not provide the same protection against magic as iron, but provides an ideal defense against the blows of sword or mace. Steel armor can absorb 3 Notches.

- Steel armor takes up 3 belt slots while worn.

- Due to steel’s weight, it is impossible to swim or sneak while wearing steel armor.

- Even though steel armor does not cancel targeted spells, you cannot cast while wearing it due to the latent iron content in the metal.

name: Bezoar
properties: Swallow: counts as 8 burned charges toward curing an affliction
description: Bezoars are stones composed of hair, vegetable material, and bile found in the stomachs of goats. If swallowed, a bezoar acts as a powerful anti-poison. It counts as 8 burned charges for the purposes of curing an affliction while Resting and Recovering.

name: Blank Book
description: Blank books are useful for taking notes or even composing a memoir for later publication.

name: Booze, Fancy
description: Booze is any alcohol that’s nice, strong, and expensive. This can be traded to Underworld denizens, given as a gift to a noble, or opened for a special occasion. Weak alcohol like small beer, cider, or watery wine is considered to be part of a ration.

name: Clothes, Finery
description: Finery sets you apart from the general rabble. Be careful, though; adventuring has a tendency to make you dirty and bloody.

name: Falconry Gear
description: Falconry gear includes gloves, tethers, hoods, and everything needed to enjoy the royal sport of hawking.

name: Hourglass
description: Hourglasses are one of the best ways to track time in the strange Underworld. Even then, adventurers will notice that time flows curiously. An hour in the Underworld might be a day in the City.

name: Lantern
properties: Bright light for the holder, dim for the guild
Dropped: destroyed
Belt-tied: shatters (and oils you) if you take a Wound
Burns oil: 4 flickers per flask
description: Lanterns provide bright light to the person holding them and dim light for everybody else in the guild. Lanterns are lit by oil, which must be carried separately. Lanterns shatter when they are dropped. You can tie a lantern to your belt, but if you take a Wound the lantern shatters and you’re covered in oil. A lantern can flicker 4 times before their oil is used up.

name: Manacles
description: Manacles are used to restrain people, usually to haul them back to the City for a reward.

name: Oil, Flask
properties: Fuels a lantern (4 flickers)
description: Oil, usually carried in a flask, is used to fuel lanterns. It can also fuel other fires, or be used as lubrication. A flask of oil provides 4 flickers to a lantern.

name: Shield, Heavy
properties: Held: ties in Challenges go to you, not the attacker
May swap Initiative for a hand card (miscellaneous action)
Absorbs 2 Notches
description:
Shields come in a variety of styles and methods of construction, but are considered either light or heavy. Shields take one of your hand slots.

- Light shields are made from wood. They can absorb 1 Notch.

- Heavy shields are made of metal or similarly sturdy materials and can absorb 2 Notches.

In a Challenge, ties go to the attacker unless the defender carries a shield.

If you have a shield, you may replace your Initiative with any card from your hand as a miscellaneous action. Your old Initiative is discarded.

name: Spyglass
properties: Magnifies up to 5×
description: Spyglasses magnify things up to five times.

name: Tent
properties: Shelters two — counts toward shelter at camp
description: Tents provide shelter and comfortable sleeping for up to two people. Without a bedroll or tent, sleeping on the cold ground makes you Stressed. (Oversized)

name: Wand of Archwood
properties: With Gramarye: a missile weapon of magical blasts (up to one zone)
description: Wands of archwood are ritual implements that allow sorcerers to focus their magical potential. If you have the Gramarye talent, you may use an archwood wand as a missile weapon to create blasts of magical energy up to a zone away.

name: Weaponry, Silver
properties: Effective against lycanthropes, spirits, and certain undead
Soft: absorbs only 1 Notch
description:
Weaponry includes any basic arms and ammo listed in the Weapons section on the following page.

- Silver weaponry is expensive, but silver is often useful against lycanthropes, spirits, and certain types of undead. Silver weaponry is soft and easily damaged, however. Silver weapons can only absorb 1 Notch before being Destroyed.
